paragraphs: What did Reuters say Lionel Messi did in Argentina's two key attacking moments?

Reuters said Messi provided the assist for Enzo Fernandez's equalizer. It also said Messi supplied the cross that led to Lautaro Martinez's winning goal. These details put Messi at the center of two attacks, although he was not the player who scored either goal.

That creates an important question in this comeback story. Should the main focus be on the players who finished the chances, or on the player Reuters said created them? Both views describe important parts of the same result.

Argentina came from behind to defeat England 2-1 in the World Cup semifinal on July 15, 2026. Anthony Gordon scored England's opening goal. Argentina then advanced to the World Cup final against Spain.

In a match report, one goal can involve more than one important player. One player may make a pass or a cross, while another player scores. Listeners need to notice which role the report is describing.

subsections.heading: The Chance Creator
paragraphs: Reuters separates Messi's contribution from the final touch. In its account, he assisted Fernandez's equalizer and then delivered the cross leading to Martinez's winning goal.

The word "assist" tells listeners that Messi set up the equalizer. The description of a cross tells us about the move before the winning finish. This is why Messi can be highlighted in the comeback, based on Reuters' report.
His role, as Reuters described it, was to create the two key attacking actions. That is a major part of the story, even though the goals were scored by other players.

subsections.heading: The Goal Finishers
paragraphs: Yet the scoring record points to Fernandez and Martinez. Fernandez equalized for Argentina, and Martinez scored the winning goal in the 92nd minute. They were the players who turned the two attacks into goals.

This is a different part of the story. Reuters credited Messi with creating the chances, but it did not say he scored. The scorers and the creator can both be highlighted without treating their roles as the same.
So, the comeback has two kinds of key contribution: making the opportunity and finishing it. Fernandez and Martinez represent the finishing side of the report.

sections.heading: In One Line
paragraphs: Reuters' account divides the comeback into two roles: Messi created the equalizer and the winning move, while Fernandez and Martinez scored the goals.
That distinction can change how listeners understand a match report. It can name the player who made an opportunity and the player who completed it.
Argentina's 2-1 win sent the team to the final against Spain.
